name: Heidi Ganahl
subject: The Mountain Minute: 1/10/2025
body:
House Republicans launch 23 policy positions in 25-second video to open session
| | | | [](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
---
**PRESENTED BY[ROCKYMOUNTAINVOICE.COM](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
| | Happy Friday!
Here is a summary of today's newsletter, plus some other articles we think you might be interested in reading:
🏛️ **Colorado Legislative Session Updates...** The 75th Colorado General Assembly opened with an eventful day of ceremonies, spirited debates, and the introduction of significant legislation. House Republicans’ outlined [23 policy goals](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) in a 25-second video released today, focusing on affordability, TABOR protections, public safety, and rural advocacy. Meanwhile, opening-day bills from Democrats tackled issues like unionization, wage theft, and price gouging, promising a [contentious session ahead](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.). Observers from the gallery, including representatives of [various labor and special interest groups](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.), set the tone for an active and closely watched legislative term. ****
******🏔️ Here in Colorado...** Colorado Parks and Wildlife [rejected a petition](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) to pause the reintroduction of wolves on a 10-1 vote, pushing forward despite calls for a delay to address local concerns. Meanwhile, Montrose councilman Anthony Mijares is making waves with his [bipartisan approach](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.), leveraging his whiskey bar roots to bridge divides in his new role. Lastly, Colorado mayors are urging state leaders [not to strip local powers](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) as crime rises.****
🇺🇸**On the National Stage...** A federal court struck down President Biden's attempt to [rewrite Title IX](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) to allow biological males in women’s sports and spaces. Los Angeles faced criticism after [$17.6 million in fire department budget cuts](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) and stolen fire hydrants exacerbated the city’s challenges during recent wildfires.
🗣️ **Commentary Spotlight...** C.J. Garbo critiques Mark Zuckerberg's quiet retreat from Facebook’s fact-checking program, framing it as an [admission of failure](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) without accountability. A letter to RMV from one of our readers reflects on how media coverage of Jimmy Carter’s funeral failed to respect his legacy with [unnecessary, biased commentary](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) throughout.
This is the Mountain Minute for Friday, January 10, 2025.
– Heidi Ganahl
---
|
---
**TODAY'S TOP STORIES**
**[House Republicans launch 23 policy positions in 25-second video to open session](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** BY BRIAN PORTER, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
If Republican House Minority Leader Rose Pugliese did not make it clear enough Wednesday in her address to the Colorado House upon the opening of the 75th General Assembly, the caucus simplified her words.
In a 25-second video also released Wednesday, the caucus shared 23 items which it pledges to address during the session.
The first item was the topic of the day on Wednesday: affordability.
“This session, the House Republicans remain steadfast in addressing the issues of importance to Coloradans,” she said. “Affordability remains at the forefront of our discussions. You will see that in our legislation.”
**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
The Rocky Mountain Voice is a 501(c)(3) that exists to promote informed civic engagement and the principles of self-governance through enterprise reporting on public policy, politics, government, and culture throughout the state of Colorado. The only way we can continue to spread truth is with your support!
[DONATE TO ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
|
---
**[Opening-day bills tackle unionization, wage theft, price gouging, Ozempic and more](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [THE SUM & SUBSTANCE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
* Colorado's legislative session began with the introduction of 126 bills, tackling issues like unionization, wage theft, price gouging, and health care. High-profile proposals include repealing a second vote requirement for union fee deductions and increased penalties for wage theft.
* Affordability measures are a major focus, with bills addressing price gouging on essential items, reducing childcare fees, and repealing the state's 10-cent bag fee.
* Other notable proposals include bipartisan support for nuclear energy, tax incentives for attracting film festivals, and regulatory changes to encourage modular housing construction.

**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
**[Hardin: A glance from the gallery on the start of the 75th legislative session](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
* The 75th Colorado General Assembly opened with a ceremonial start, featuring a land acknowledgment by Ernest House Jr., patriotic displays, and a large gathering of attendees, including the Ute Mountain Ute Tribe, Teamsters, bikers, and other special interest groups.
* With more than 100 bills already introduced, lawmakers are expected to tackle major topics like firearms legislation, public safety, and economic measures in the months ahead. Public engagement is encouraged as the session unfolds.

**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
**[CPW Commission denies petition to pause wolf introduction on 10-1 vote](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
* The Colorado Parks and Wildlife Commission rejected a petition to pause the state’s wolf introduction program on a 10-1 vote, despite widespread calls from ranchers and agricultural organizations for delay until mitigation measures were fully functional.
* The six-hour hearing featured emotional testimony from nearly 100 individuals, with sharp criticism directed at ranchers by some advocates and accusations of falsified wolf damage claims. Opponents of the pause argued the program is essential for ecological restoration.
* Commissioner Marie Haskett, the lone vote in favor of the pause, expressed concerns about incomplete mitigation tools, including insufficiently trained guardian dogs and unready range rider programs. However, the majority opted to proceed as planned.

**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
**[Mijares bridges Montrose divides with whiskey bar, now hopes to do so in office](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
* Newly elected Montrose County Commissioner Scott Mijares draws on his experience running a local whiskey bar to foster bipartisan dialogue, emphasizing community values and collaboration.
* Mijares plans to enhance government transparency by creating an accessible online platform for county information and prioritizing grassroots decision-making to rebuild trust in local governance.
* Addressing concerns about underfunding in the sheriff’s department, Mijares aims to ensure that public safety remains a top priority, reflecting his commitment to protecting Montrose County’s heritage and residents.

**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
**ONLY ON ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE ⤵️**
**[‘We need our roads fixed’: ‘Peltonia’ Sen. Byron Pelton opens 75th session with reminder for Gov. Polis](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** **BY BRIAN PORTER | STATE | JANUARY 8** _Sen. Byron Pelton, R-Sterling, claims to have already been at work for his constituency as the 75th General Assembly opened Wednesday._
**[Minority Leader Pugliese pledges Republicans will find solutions for all Coloradans in 75th session](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** **BY BRIAN PORTER****| STATE | JANUARY 8** _When Rose Pugliese left behind her children to begin the 75th General Assembly in Denver, as children will do she was asked why she did the job of Colorado House minority leader._
**[One early moment in 75th Legislature demonstrates unity of House Republican caucus](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)****BY BRIAN PORTER****| STATE | JANUARY 8** _What’s the difference between the Republican caucus in the Colorado House of Representatives and the U.S. House?_ _Unity. On Wednesday, as the Colorado House opened the 75th General Assembly, Republicans displayed unity by unanimously supporting Minority Leader Rose Pugliese to become the Colorado House Speaker._ __

**Feel like you have a unique story or commentary to share?** We'd love to talk with you about becoming an RMV Contributor. Reply to this email (or reach out to [brian@rockymountainvoice.com](mailto:brian@rockymountainvoice.com?subject=Contributor%20Interest%20\(via%20Mountain%20Minute\))) or send us a message on social media to chat!
|
---
|
---
**🤔 DID YOU KNOW?** In 1977, Jimmy Carter became the first president to walk from the Capitol to the White House during the**inaugural parade** , defying Secret Service advice and establishing a tradition followed by every president since.
|
---
**CARTOON OF THE DAY**
|
---
**QUOTE OF THE DAY** "You may have to fight a battle more than once to win it." — Margaret Thatcher
|
---

| | | | |

| | [](https://qgzxgy5ab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001VyjmeCC2u_ns_MVs_GwayVKSVkIVK5SLKCFDgSECLc3UFY5iPIR9OGSf0ZsH7PWMfIsTZgbI8y4M-WR4enG1GBGpB9dWSVUc5fkqj0bYPUNVVbr1IKylilZOq2Ax3sFqnCRk1feG2Jsyk5cxRgxzCA==&c=ixsgRK9Byg-bPkARGWz-hpMMMLRnXVjfvDCluyUFquFWVCwO1Ok8BA==&ch=tohrMyfTbSfrd_YhaApfDrEC5qKKSt5zT-H0TvEFLQf32lfDcGVeNA==)
---
| MassGOP Joins House Minority Leader Brad Jones in Condemning Governor Healey's Handling of Migrant Crisis, Demand Immediate Action
---
---
| Boston, MA-- The Massachusetts Republican Party stands united with [House Minority Leader Brad Jones](https://qgzxgy5ab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001VyjmeCC2u_ns_MVs_GwayVKSVkIVK5SLKCFDgSECLc3UFY5iPIR9OJo_CdL5cWWcZKH-N8DcT27DWhJj3jnNgtNvMX8HutDMYPu7OUjGyQfRIHdZvtoRZkYDycXejJjaLy6pnpqnSiYke5s9pRucLAAtLn3TWr0PVO1Ie_wT-aUPGaD-7iTxwZyaas4k6jBD6RIg4-EvZF8cs4hZAtJzuFypVhyqe4NAOLmF6Pkl-WnkmHpSjEhyaQ==&c=ixsgRK9Byg-bPkARGWz-hpMMMLRnXVjfvDCluyUFquFWVCwO1Ok8BA==&ch=tohrMyfTbSfrd_YhaApfDrEC5qKKSt5zT-H0TvEFLQf32lfDcGVeNA==) in expressing outrage over what appears to be dishonesty or gross negligence on the part of Governor Maura Healey. Recent revelations have exposed that migrants entering state-funded shelters in Massachusetts are not subjected to CORI background checks--despite the Governor's previous claims that her administration was conducting "extensive background checks" on applicants seeking shelter assistance.
MassGOP Chair Amy Carnevale issued a blistering statement, calling into question the Governor's integrity and competency:
"Either the Governor has been dishonest with Massachusetts residents, or she is oblivious to how her administration is mismanaging the migrant crisis. A bombshell FOIA request revealed over 1,000 serious incidents in state shelters since 2022. These include nearly a dozen allegations of rape or sexual assault and at least 170 incidents of domestic violence. The fact that the Governor only now feels compelled to act, after these horrors have come to light, is despicable."
"The legislature must stand united and demand urgent reforms to the state's right-to-shelter law. Massachusetts taxpayers cannot continue to fund a broken system that perpetuates this humanitarian disaster. The time for excuses is over."
The MassGOP calls on Governor Healey and legislative leaders to prioritize the safety of all residents and migrants by implementing a six month residency requirement to qualify for emergency housing along with robust reforms to ensure accountability, transparency, and effective management of the state's shelter system.
###

name: Susanna Gibson
subject: You’ll hear more stories like mine this year…
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Her Bold Move Action
body:
And it’s the reason many women don’t pursue public office. | | |
---
| | [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/100148250/521912179/1701071684?refcode=EM_F_25010stories_N_SG_90DO_16_G_N_Logo&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNy8xLzg4NDk1IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjVkODYxMzQwLTViY2YtZWYxMS04OGQwLTAwMjI0ODJhOWQ5MiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=Xa9gbApWG4IUjk3ksVlcVa8f03j1WP0VxcfWINMwUmM=)
---
Hi Peter,

As we enter another year in which instances of non-consensual image-based abuse continue to rise, I wanted to reach out and make sure you saw my story.

Her Bold Move endorsed me while I was running for a State Delegate seat in Virginia. The polls showed I was on track to win in a Republican-leaning district – **until my campaign was the target of a uniquely sexist attack.**

**Before I tell you more, I want to emphasize that Her Bold Move stepped up when too many other organizations were afraid to publicly stand by me.** [**Now, they need your help to ensure they can provide that same game-changing support to other candidates running for office this year. Will you join me in uplifting this extraordinary organization by pitching in $20.25 or more today?**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/100148255/521912269/1701071684?refcode=EM_F_25010stories_N_SG_90DO_16_G_N_L1&amount=20.25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNy8xLzg4NDk1IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjVkODYxMzQwLTViY2YtZWYxMS04OGQwLTAwMjI0ODJhOWQ5MiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=Xa9gbApWG4IUjk3ksVlcVa8f03j1WP0VxcfWINMwUmM=)

Here is what happened:

In the final weeks of my run for office, I found out I had been the target of nonconsensual pornography, nonconsensual distribution of intimate images, and severe image-based sexual abuse both online and in person.

In fact, the Republican Party of Virginia – yes, _the official state Republican party_ – sent out explicit photos of me to thousands of homes in my district. My neighbors’ homes.

**Here is a look at what I went through in the wake of these attacks:**

❌ I received hundreds of phone calls and social media messages that included threats of rape and death.
❌ My children had to have security stay with them at school for weeks.
❌ I was forced to leave my job as a nurse practitioner.
❌ I had to leave the state to escape reporters and threatening strangers showing up at my house.
❌ I was unable to meet with voters for three weeks during the height of election season.

**Male candidates do not experience this type of sexual harassment or abuse when they run for office.**

[**But here’s the thing: now more than ever before, we need women to run for office. Stories like mine are the reason many women don’t. Chip in today to help change that.**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/100148264/521912294/1701071684?refcode=EM_F_25010stories_N_SG_90DO_16_G_N_L2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNy8xLzg4NDk1IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjVkODYxMzQwLTViY2YtZWYxMS04OGQwLTAwMjI0ODJhOWQ5MiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=Xa9gbApWG4IUjk3ksVlcVa8f03j1WP0VxcfWINMwUmM=)

I’m writing this email today to spread an important message to any woman considering a run for office: Her Bold Move is an amazing resource. I hope every woman candidate will take advantage of what HBM can offer.

They understood that their role in my campaign had become more important than ever – that I was the victim of a _sexist attack_ reserved for _women candidates._ They renewed their support for me and encouraged other endorsers to follow their lead, they checked in on me constantly, they helped me fundraise when institutions were actually pulling funding from my campaign. At one of the loneliest times of my life, the HBM team made me feel less alone.

It’s clear their mission to fight misogyny shows up in their actions as an org – not just their words.

**Despite everything I outlined above – I was only 715 votes shy of winning that seat in the Virginia House of Delegates. It’s frustrating to imagine how the outcome might have been different if more orgs had followed Her Bold Move’s lead. We could have avoided electing one more anti-choice legislator in my state.**

[**I am so grateful for the work that Her Bold Move is doing to defend and empower women like me. Will you join me in supporting this important organization by pitching in $20.25 or more today?**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/100148270/521912329/1701071684?refcode=EM_F_25010stories_N_SG_90DO_16_G_N_L3&amount=20.25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNy8xLzg4NDk1IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjVkODYxMzQwLTViY2YtZWYxMS04OGQwLTAwMjI0ODJhOWQ5MiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=Xa9gbApWG4IUjk3ksVlcVa8f03j1WP0VxcfWINMwUmM=) _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | [**$20.25 »**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/100148279/521912386/1701071684?refcode=EM_F_25010stories_N_SG_90DO_16_G_N_EB20.25&express_lane=true&amount=20.25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNy8xLzg4NDk1IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjVkODYxMzQwLTViY2YtZWYxMS04OGQwLTAwMjI0ODJhOWQ5MiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=Xa9gbApWG4IUjk3ksVlcVa8f03j1WP0VxcfWINMwUmM=)

Running for office as a woman can be dangerous and scary. The political landscape is a safer and more supportive place for women with Her Bold Move at the forefront.

Thank you,

Susanna Gibson

name: All On The Line
subject: We’d like to hear from you on this
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE NATIONAL REDISTRICTING ACTION FUND
body:
This community will pick our next in-depth update.
|
---

Peter,
With so much of the oxygen going towards our chaotic national politics, we know it can be hard to find up-to-date, reliable, and comprehensive coverage on what’s happening on the ground in state-level fights for fair maps and redistricting reform.
But we know it’s important you have the information you need to stand up for democracy — especially with the incoming administration.
**So later this month, our team is going to provide a closer look into a key state — and it’s up to this community to decide which one.**[**Click here to cast your vote and help us decide which state we should highlight in our next in-depth analysis.**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/100150816/521919608/593034927?refcode=eng_aotl_tier1_email_CommunityPollSrvy_email_20250110-AOTL-Tier1-CommunityPollSrvy_&utm_source=email&utm_medium=email&utm_term=eng&utm_content=20250110-AOTL-Tier1-CommunityPollSrvy_&utm_campaign=CommunityPollSrvy&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk2IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjY3ODYxMzQwLTViY2YtZWYxMS04OGQwLTAwMjI0ODJhOWQ5MiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=WqG_FvdAfr-K1efn_BGqYJEoXLnOgodPwJTC5v-Plfs=&emci=46e11e41-c1ce-ef11-88d0-0022482a9d92&emdi=67861340-5bcf-ef11-88d0-0022482a9d92&ceid=8431045)
We’ll be looking at the current balance of power in the state legislature and upcoming elections; the state’s redistricting process, including the history of how we got to where we are today and the fairness of the current maps; and timelines of upcoming redistricting battles — and what’s at stake with them.
As you know, there is no “off season” in redistricting. Gerrymandering continues to pose a critical threat to our democracy, with opponents of fair maps _actively_ undermining redistricting reforms, electing anti-democracy leaders, and threatening mid-decade map redraws. **But you don’t always hear about it. **
[**So don’t wait: Cast your vote right now to help us decide which key state should be featured in our next comprehensive update.**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/100150817/521919620/593034927?refcode=eng_aotl_tier1_email_CommunityPollSrvy_email_20250110-AOTL-Tier1-CommunityPollSrvy_&utm_source=email&utm_medium=email&utm_term=eng&utm_content=20250110-AOTL-Tier1-CommunityPollSrvy_&utm_campaign=CommunityPollSrvy&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk2IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjY3ODYxMzQwLTViY2YtZWYxMS04OGQwLTAwMjI0ODJhOWQ5MiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=WqG_FvdAfr-K1efn_BGqYJEoXLnOgodPwJTC5v-Plfs=&emci=46e11e41-c1ce-ef11-88d0-0022482a9d92&emdi=67861340-5bcf-ef11-88d0-0022482a9d92&ceid=8431045)
Thanks for your input — it matters a lot.

name: Alan Dershowitz
subject: The New York Sun is Different
body:
It’s a paper that places principle over politics and people over party.
And we need you to join us.

# FROM THE DESK OF ALAN DERSHOWITZ
---
| | Dear Reader In over sixty years in the legal profession, I’ve experienced first hand the impact of both good journalism and bad journalism — mostly the latter — and just how important and rare it is to get things right.

**In today’s media landscape there are far too many publications that get things wrong.** Many are more concerned with the political implications of a story than the basic facts.

**The New York Sun is different.** It’s a paper that places principle over politics and people over party.

Its in-depth coverage of the legal challenges faced by President Trump serve as a case in point.

When I defended President Trump during his first impeachment, I faced an avalanche of criticism, but few papers made sufficient effort to fully appreciate the significance of the legal arguments.

The Sun’s deeply knowledgeable leadership, including its editor Seth Lipsky and associate editor A.R. Hoffman are themselves students of the Constitution. It shows in their coverage, which I find to be first rate.

It will be no secret to you that I don’t agree with everything that can be found in the Sun’s pages, but I can say with confidence that its re-emergence is among the most important recent developments in American journalism.

I’ve been impressed by the Sun’s growth so far, but I know it faces an uphill battle, one that I have pledged to join.

**The Sun’s success will be a gift to the American people and to the cause of truth.** But to succeed, we need you to join us. I highly encourage you to join the Sun today.

Every person matters in this effort.

Don’t wait. The battle to restore American journalism starts now.
---

It will be no secret to you that I don’t agree with everything that can be found in the Sun’s pages, but I can say with confidence that its re-emergence is among the most important recent developments in American journalism. I’ve been impressed by the Sun’s growth so far, but I know it faces an uphill battle, one that I have pledged to join. The Sun’s success will be a gift to the American people and to the cause of truth. But to succeed, we need you to join us. I highly encourage you to join the Sun today. Every person matters in this effort. Don’t wait. The battle to restore American journalism starts now. | | | | | Alan Dershowitz Columnist for The New York Sun | | | | | Alan Dershowitz Columnist for The New York Sun | | |

| | | About Blue Amp Action: We cannot sugarcoat the current situation. Right-wing authoritarianism, defeated by a worldwide coalition during WWII, was only in hibernation. Over the past decade it has infected democratic societies--including ours. It is as simple as it is terrifying: What we do now will define the future of our country and world. We at Blue Amp Action are dedicated to liberal, open societies, where reason and science are applied to solve our problems; where civil rights--including voting rights!--are protected for all. We fight for these values every day. Speaking up on our platforms, raising the voices and the political prospects of liberal pols and pundits. **And savvy grassroots activists like you know, just like we do, that we can’t stop watching the people we help elect:_ after the elections, we have to keep organizing support for the policies we like and pushing back against the ones we don’t._** **__When the public tunes out between elections and the only people our elected officials hear from are the paid lobbyists of wealthy corporations, our government stops being accountable to the needs of ordinary families.__** **Democracy never sleeps, and neither can we.** So we ask that you support our work, so we can continue to engage in this fight every moment of every day. So our kids grow up in democracy, not dystopia. Thank you for your support for all we do! Please follow Cliff Schecter on Twitter @cliffschecter, or subscribe to The Takedown with Cliff Schecter on YouTube, to keep up to date with this battle for the heart and soul of our country. — _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. Americans are more optimistic about the economy than they were before Donald Trump’s reelection, a newly released survey from pollster Scott Rasmussen’s Napolitan News Service shows. CNN Trump supporter Scott Jennings **[SNAPPED at a democrat dummy](https://substack.com/redirect/62958032-c50c-4b74-9584-4768bf570f64?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** for mocking
Donald Trump's agenda to make Greenland part of the U.S.A.
[](https://substack.com/redirect/62958032-c50c-4b74-9584-4768bf570f64?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
"So you're for attacking Greenland Scott, that's what your'e saying?!" asked Josh Rogin of The Washington Post.
"No!" Jennings fired back. "We don't need to attack Greenland, we can buy it!"
Jennings further explained that Trump is "thinking big" about making global diplomatic moves in America's interest.
Watch the two scrap right here:
[Watch Video](https://substack.com/redirect/62958032-c50c-4b74-9584-4768bf570f64?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Trump's interest in acquiring Greenland is driven by several factors. Greenland's geographical location in the Arctic makes it crucial for U.S. national security, especially with respect to threats from Russia and China.
Greenland is also rich in natural resources like minerals and possibly vast reserves of oil and gas which could be critical for U.S. economic interests.
The move would further Trump's "America First" agenda, aiming to peacefully expand U.S. territory and influence in ways not seen since the late 19th century.

# _MAGA Moments:_ Lara Trump DESTROYS Dana Bash when she tries disrespecting her family on live tv
Watch Lara Trump **[shut CNN's Dana Bash right up](https://substack.com/redirect/f564c783-eedb-4a23-9c58-5e2c8803c3e0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** after she drags her father-in-law, Donald, through the mud on live T.V.
[](https://substack.com/redirect/f564c783-eedb-4a23-9c58-5e2c8803c3e0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Bash began listing several examples of "misinformation" she says Donald Trump has pushed about relief for the hurricane disasters in North Carolina.
"Why is he spreading misinformation in a crisis like that?" Bash asked Trump, specifically refuting a claim that "the administration is redirecting disaster money for undocumented immigrants."
Lara was ready for this one...
"The idea that we spent $650 million in fiscal years 2024 on the migrant crisis that Kamala Harris was responsible for stopping, and by all accounts, she created having an open door policy at our southern border, is infuriating!" Trump exclaimed.
[Watch Video](https://substack.com/redirect/f564c783-eedb-4a23-9c58-5e2c8803c3e0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
While it may be true that organizations like FEMA did not "divert" money from helping American citizens to helping illegal migrants, the agency openly has billion dollar budgets already approved by congress which it spends yearly towards housing and assisting illegal migrants across the country. This while American citizens struggled to obtain the resources they needed to survive in the aftermath of hurricane Helene.
"The simple truth is that FEMA's spending reflects its DEI agenda. FEMA spent $1 billion on illegal alien services instead of disaster relief resources that could have mitigated the deadly impact of recent hurricanes," **[writes Brandy Perez,](https://substack.com/redirect/074abf8d-4619-4a6d-ba26-1ca716105ead?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** border security research assistant for the Heritage Foundation.

name: Diane Sare
subject: Friday Symposium: Nationalize the Federal Reserve!
body:
| | [](https://laroucheorganization.nationbuilder.com/r?u=6U-bS5myFB-qaXONaTKygW8Bw5o4M8XoHC3TxaP90HPeLIFflw7WCajMEVGvmb9S&e=8fa7b86c85d8b09f0015ebe544605791&utm_source=laroucheorganization&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=20240110_symposium&n=1)
---
| Friend – There can be no real economic recovery in the United States as long as the private Federal Reserve system continues to function as an open spigot to bail out trillions upon trillions of dollars of worthless speculative paper. Since the 2007 crash, Federal Reserve policy has not only supported a decade of economic stagnation and runaway inflation but, since late-2019, has also supported the U.S. Treasury in the greatest flood of debt, deficit and general government subsidy in the history of any nation. Congress and the President must now act together to end this insanity. The Federal Reserve Bank should be nationalized and given a new, radically different charter, mandating it to cease functioning as a reserve bank for big banks, and, instead, become a credit institution for the nation’s capital budget which would stimulate capital goods exports for economic development. This week's symposium features _Executive Intelligence Review_ economics experts Paul Gallagher and Richard Freeman who will lay out precisely how this can be done, and how an actual economic renaissance can be created in the United States.
* * *
### [ Friday Symposium, January 10, 2024, 8:00 PM](https://laroucheorganization.nationbuilder.com/r?u=2fVGP-PhvXIwVfYRj2FCFqngN2zX8pyoXFMsgyDLIFn9DFMnp5oiC05e0qPZ_PH9nlf92SntbbqyR_pzDVipfg&e=8fa7b86c85d8b09f0015ebe544605791&utm_source=laroucheorganization&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=20240110_symposium&n=2)

name: Gay Valimont
subject: Our Democracy is thanks to President Carter ; Let’s honor him.
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Gay Valimont Gay Valimont For Congress
body:
| | | Dear friend, Yesterday we mourned the death of the 39th President of the United States, and fellow Democrat, Jimmy Carter.**** **It is up to us to honor his efforts.**
---
---
Hello, my name is Gay Valimont, running for FL-1 in the upcoming special election. My mission is to honor the memory of the great Democrat that fought so hard to help this country by aiding our Congress in passing meaningful legislation. **We want you to be a part of this.** That said, the tragic passing of President Carter highlights some of the immaturity within our very own government, and we believe there is no better way to commemorate the efforts of this courageous individual than to spread the word about our mission: **to maintain a high functioning democracy**. His unending commitment to diplomacy and humanity is an inspiration to us all, and a constant reminder of the power of democracy. Sadly, the recent milestone President Carter hit turning 100 years old was _not_ celebrated by President-elect Trump, a disappointing endeavor considering his tragic passing a few short months after.
---
---
In the wake of this loss, I strive to look forward, and honor the righteous memory of those who have paved the way for us to get this far as a democracy.
**[Supporting our mission is as simple as donating $9 today, to ensure our campaign stays on track.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/100163452/521940397/181761264?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDc3NDYiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZDEzMTE2YTUtNjNjZi1lZjExLTg4ZDAtMDAyMjQ4MmE5ZDkyIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=1DfrkYLlq9j5iF6K9USzU47G81tm8hACbvb3oyyFUro=)**

name: Elissa Slotkin for Michigan
subject: Memo: Elissa Slotkin’s Winning Blueprint
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ELISSA SLOTKIN FOR MICHIGAN
body:
Sharing something unprecedented
# [](https://act.elissaslotkin.org/go/188?t=1&akid=2224%2E135344%2EZ29xzf)

Elissa Slotkin's victory was the most expensive campaign in Michigan history and one of the closest as well.
As a supporter of her campaign, we want to do something pretty unprecedented -- share with you the deep dive into the dynamics that made victory possible.
But before we do, we want to ask you to make a monthly recurring donation to her re-election. We do have some fundraising goals still and don't want to send a million emails asking for money. Getting a bunch of recurring donations banked will help us do that. And every time it hits, just know you're doing more than almost anyone to help ensure Elissa's long-term success.
[**Make a small monthly recurring donation here.**](https://act.elissaslotkin.org/go/1960?c0=ebs-em-jan25.mLp_Iw&refcode=em2501XX-1-XX&t=2&akid=2224%2E135344%2EZ29xzf)
**[CONTRIBUTE NOW](https://act.elissaslotkin.org/go/1960?c0=ebs-em-jan25.mLp_Iw&refcode=em2501XX-1-XX&t=3&akid=2224%2E135344%2EZ29xzf)**
Here's the press release, and the full memo linked below:
**SUBJECT** : Elissa Slotkin’s Winning Blueprint
**TO** : dpwillis67@gmail.com
**FROM** : Matt Hennessey, Campaign Manager

* * *
With Michigan’s 2024 election now formally certified, U.S. Senator-elect Elissa Slotkin’s campaign today released a memo highlighting her path to victory.
The 2024 Senate campaign was the most expensive in Michigan’s history, with over $50 million being spent by outside groups alone attacking Slotkin. Slotkin is only the second candidate in Michigan’s history to win a U.S. Senate race while the party’s presidential candidate lost the state.
Among the campaign memo’s key tactical and strategic findings:
* **Slotkin’s victory was enabled by split ticket voters.**
* Slotkin earned more raw votes than VP Kamala Harris in 22 counties, including vote-rich Macomb, Genesee, Saginaw, Livingston and Ingham counties.
* Slotkin was the top Democratic vote-getter in these 22 counties. While some have pointed to an “undervote” -- voters who cast ballots for president but not in the Senate race – Michigan’s 2024 election saw a standard undervote, relative to comparable elections.
* This means that, across the state, tens of thousands of voters split their ticket and voted for President-elect Trump and Slotkin. This is not a new phenomenon for Slotkin: In the 2020 election, she was one of seven House Democrats to win a congressional district carried by President Trump.
* **Slotkin’s overperformance was decisive in counties with concentrations of working class voters.**
* Slotkin overperformed Harris in 68 of Michigan’s 83 counties.
* This was most notable in Macomb County, where Slotkin outperformed Harris by more than 21,000 votes, as well as Downriver in Wayne County (Slotkin +2.2% relative to Harris); Genesee County (Slotkin +3.5%); and Saginaw County (Slotkin +3.2%).
* Slotkin outperformed Harris in 63 of the 70 most rural counties in the state; this overperformance netted Slotkin more than 38,000 votes over the top of the ticket.
[](https://act.elissaslotkin.org/go/9?c0=ebs-em-jan25.tDItk9&refcode=em2501XX-1-XX&t=4&akid=2224%2E135344%2EZ29xzf)
* **Slotkin’s paid media strategy,** emphasizing her strong biography and record of bipartisan service while highlighting Republican opponent Mike Rogers and his harmful record, helped establish her distinct brand and define Rogers. Her personal story connected with Michigan voters, creating a distinct brand separate from the national party. The campaign’s decision to introduce her to voters early in the election cycle provided a key advantage, allowing Slotkin’s brand to take root before other spenders could define her.
* That effective strategy was critical because of the flood of spending by Republican groups in the race. Super-PACs and other outside Republican spenders poured $78.2 million into Michigan propping up Rogers. In only three Senate races did GOP groups spend more – Ohio, Pennsylvania and Montana, all involving Democratic incumbents. While Slotkin’s strong brand and small-donor support allowed her to significantly outraise Rogers individually, total spending only slightly favored Slotkin, $113 million to $91 million.

The full analysis memo is [**attached.**](https://act.elissaslotkin.org/go/3584?t=5&akid=2224%2E135344%2EZ29xzf)

PAID FOR BY ELISSA SLOTKIN FOR MICHIGAN
P.O. Box 4145
East Lansing, MI 48826
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a very important way we communicate with supporters like you, but if you want to, you can [ unsubscribe here](https://act.elissaslotkin.org/unsubscribe/unsubscribe?t=6&akid=2224%2E135344%2EZ29xzf).

[ Sign up here to receive fewer emails.](https://act.elissaslotkin.org/signup/users-fewer-emails/?t=7&akid=2224%2E135344%2EZ29xzf)

Elissa Slotkin served in the Central Intelligence Agency and the Department of Defense. Use of her job titles and photographs during service do not imply endorsement by the Central Intelligence Agency OR the Department of Defense.

Solicitations made by Elissa Slotkin are limited by Federal law. As a Federal candidates and officeholder she is soliciting only donations of up to $3300 per election from individuals and up to $5000 per election from multi-candidate political committees. She are not soliciting donations in any amount from corporations, labororganizations, foreign nationals, federal contractors, or national banks.

subject: 🚨BREAKING: DeVore Sues McCombie
body:
Alleging a violation of First Amendment rights, former Republican nominee for IL AG Tom DeVore filed a lawsuit against IL GOP Leader Tony McCombie.

We thought you may be interested to receive this conservative news alert from Illinois Review, the largest conservative media source in Illinois, and the only source in Illinois that has been shared by President Donald Trump.

DeVore Files Lawsuit Against McCombie, Says GOP Leader Violated First Amendment Rights by Blocking, Deleting Comments on Official Facebook Page (https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2025/01/devore-files-lawsuit-against-mccombie-says-gop-leader-violated-first-amendment-rights-by-blocking-deleting-comments-on-official-facebook-page.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=a10d145b00-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_10_30_09_24_COPY_03&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-8b40ac336f-666344823)
https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2025/01/devore-files-lawsuit-against-mccombie-says-gop-leader-violated-first-amendment-rights-by-blocking-deleting-comments-on-official-facebook-page.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=a10d145b00-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_10_30_09_24_COPY_03&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-8b40ac336f-666344823
Ill. House GOP Leader Tony McCombie. (Free Speech Photo Credit: Josh Edelson/AFP/Getty Images)
By Illinois Review

On Friday morning, former Republican nominee for Illinois Attorney General Thomas DeVore filed a lawsuit against Illinois House Republican Minority Leader Tony McCombie for violating his First Amendment rights after he made “critical comments” of the GOP leader’s vote to support expanding transgender patient rights.

The suit, which was filed in the Third Judicial Circuit, and shared exclusively with Illinois Review, states that McCombie blocked the former attorney general nominee from viewing her official Facebook page, which is a public forum and “deleted, or otherwise suppressed” his comments.

The suit mentions that other Illinois citizens have been blocked from her official page – and their critical comments have been deleted as well.
CLICK TO READ FULL ARTICLE ON FACEBOOK AND "LIKE" AND SHARE WITH YOUR FRIENDS (https://www.facebook.com/share/p/1A4LbZNPcs/?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=a10d145b00-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_10_30_09_24_COPY_03&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-8b40ac336f-666344823)
https://www.facebook.com/share/p/1QxWX2LgYx/?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=a10d145b00-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_10_30_09_24_COPY_03&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-8b40ac336f-666344823
But the controversy (https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2025/01/il-gop-leader-mccombie-votes-for-woke-patient-bill-accepts-47500-from-far-left-hospital-group-promoting-better-care-for-transgender-non-binary-individuals.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=a10d145b00-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_10_30_09_24_COPY_03&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-8b40ac336f-666344823) continues – leading up too, and after voting in favor of the bill, McCombie received an influx of campaign donations from a progressive hospital association that’s been advocating for improved care for transgender and non-binary patients.

Illinois Review editor-in-chief Mark Vargas recently discussed the controversy on the hit show, The StoneZONE with Roger Stone, where he shares co-host duties. Stone has served as a senior campaign aide to three Republican presidents – Richard Nixon, Ronald Reagan and Donald Trump and remains a close friend and ally of the 45th and 47th President of the United States. President Trump frequently shares Stone’s posts on his personal Truth Social account.

Stone has served as a senior campaign aide to three Republican presidents – Richard Nixon, Ronald Reagan and Donald Trump and remains a close friend and ally of the 45th and 47th President of the United States. President Trump frequently shares Stone’s posts on his personal Truth Social account. # [Why Is Netanyahu Protecting Hamas From Rape Prosecution?](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2550223&post_id=154426158&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTU0NDI2MTU4LCJpYXQiOjE3MzY1MjM3NTAsImV4cCI6MTczOTExNTc1MCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI1NTAyMjMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.Ft2aA0nivpobaRAwREtWM_wby6WiL9fK2kOc1xwYDvw)
### And why did 45 Democrats vote *with* MAGA Republicans to protect Netanyahu from war crimes prosecution?
| | [Qasim Rashid, Esq.](https://substack.com/@qasimrashid)
---
| Jan 10
---
| [](https://substack.com/@qasimrashid)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2550223&post_id=154426158&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTU0NDI2MTU4LCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTczNjUyMzc1MCwiZXhwIjoxNzM5MTE1NzUwLCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMjU1MDIyMyIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.CgkyA3TTVqsvNMAP1lT5_pWEM0Ev695wY61oULqFTlE&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2550223&post_id=154426158&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTU0NDI2MTU4LCJpYXQiOjE3MzY1MjM3NTAsImV4cCI6MTczOTExNTc1MCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI1NTAyMjMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.Ft2aA0nivpobaRAwREtWM_wby6WiL9fK2kOc1xwYDvw&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2550223&post_id=154426158&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTU0NDI2MTU4LCJpYXQiOjE3MzY1MjM3NTAsImV4cCI6MTczOTExNTc1MCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI1NTAyMjMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.Ft2aA0nivpobaRAwREtWM_wby6WiL9fK2kOc1xwYDvw)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9vcGVuLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9wdWIvcWFzaW1yYXNoaWQvcC93aHktaXMtbmV0YW55YWh1LXByb3RlY3RpbmctaGFtYXM_.WM_bnRvfHicgD6Tn2njc-gOH7UJ5qUCjCG7IDGMVVwg?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/qasimrashid/p/why-is-netanyahu-protecting-hamas?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

In November 2023, Israel declared there was "[overwhelming evidence](https://substack.com/redirect/6cee668a-7ecb-494b-98e3-5a7e2ac6b4e5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)" that Hamas had committed rape and sexual assault during the October 7 attacks. This narrative has since shaped the global discourse. But recent revelations have exposed unsettling truths that challenge this claim--challenges not by critics of Israel--but by Netanyahu and Israeli prosecutors themselves. The Israeli government is quietly retracting its earlier claims of mass and systemic rape, and corporate media is ignoring it altogether. Given our standard must remain absolute justice, it is critical we remain informed and get to the bottom of why Netanyahu is suddenly protecting Hamas. **Let 's Address This.**
As Legacy media continues to fail to hold the powerful accountable, your support of _Let 's Address This_ remains more critical than ever. **Please become an active member of this community and subscribe.**
[ Upgrade to paid](https://substack..YjBHh6PXgMysfq_AT3GbkZ5h6t7hRK50b98vHpmSrdg?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=154426158)
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/9f43166c-39d3-475a-add6-62f435ce4d51?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
### **The Silence of Prosecutors**
After 16 months of investigations, Israeli authorities have admitted that prosecutors have filed no allegations of rape or sexual assault against Hamas for the October 7 attacks. Prosecutor Moran Gaz is the former head of the security cases division at the Southern District Prosecutor's Office in Israel, and is a member of 'Team 7.10,' responsible for prosecuting rape and sexual assault crimes that occurred on 10/7. In an interview with Israeli media outlet Ynet News she admits that her department has "_found no evidence to substantiate the claims of "mass" and "systematic" rape by Hamas_." She want on to [state](https://substack.com/redirect/7c410df4-90c1-48c1-99d1-709018f6f668?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg):
> In the end, we don't have any complainants. What was presented in the media compared to what will eventually come together will be entirely different... We approached women's rights organisations and asked for cooperation. They told us that no one had approached them.
So, not only could her team find no evidence of mass or systemic rape, even with the help of women's rights organizations they could not find a single example to prosecute. Moran went on to state [that](https://substack.com/redirect/3c3804a0-f164-4043-b7ca-f1b656bf6ace?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg):
> [I] want to see everyone who crossed the fence stand trial and receive the death penalty. I don't have a drop of mercy. Sorry. But the path to bringing justice is slow and full of obstacles, especially with regard to sexual offenses. Unfortunately, it will be very difficult to prove them. In this matter, I would lower expectations.
How did the Israeli government go from "_overwhelming evidence_ " in November of 2023, to "_we don 't have any complainants_" after 16 months of investigations? This is a question no one in corporate media is asking, nor is it a question any American politician is demanding an answer to as the US Government continues to send billions in arms to Netanyahu. All this is in and of itself shocking, but the revelations don't end here. The Israeli government is not only refusing to act on its claims of mass and systemic rape on 10/7, they are now blocking the same UN investigations they demanded in November of 2023.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/84aedb6a-d26d-4766-99b0-19d0d578aa7b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
### **Blocking Accountability**
Israel's oldest newspaper, _Haaretz_ , reports that Israel is actively blocking the UN from investigating sexual crimes allegedly committed by Hamas on October 7. A thorough investigation could place Hamas on the UN's blacklist of entities guilty of sexual violence--a critical step toward justice. In fact, dozens of organizations in Israel and globally made this exact demand in November, 2023. Jerusalem Post [reported](https://substack.com/redirect/6cee668a-7ecb-494b-98e3-5a7e2ac6b4e5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg):
> The United Nations should declare Hamas's "[documented systematic weaponization of sexual violence](https://substack.com/redirect/958d1256-a4ab-4205-8cf6-a9fe09e51a84?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) a crime against humanity," according to a letter published Monday from more than 60 ideologically and religiously diverse American and international Jewish organizations. "Despite this overwhelming body of evidence, the UN has yet to list Hamas in the Annex to the Report of the Secretary-General as a party credibly suspected of committing or being responsible for patterns of rape or other forms of sexual violence in situations of armed conflict," the letter said.
And following up on this demand, in a March, 2024 preliminary investigation the UN reported that while there were reasonable grounds to believe acts of sexual violence had occurred, more investigations would be needed, [especially given the unexpected finding that](https://substack.com/redirect/73d79403-d3f2-469f-bfae-907beaa3d206?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg):
> While there are reasonable grounds to believe that conflict-related sexual violence occurred in the Nova music festival site, Route 232, and kibbutz Re'im, reported incidents of rape could not be verified in other locations. **Concurrently, the team determined that at least two allegations of sexual violence in kibbutz Be 'eri -- widely reported in the media -- were unfounded**. [emphasis added].
So to keep track, the narrative transitioned from "_overwhelming evidence_ " in November of 2023, to "_reasonable grounds but unfounded rape allegations_ " in March of 2024, to "_we don 't have any complainants_" in January of 2025. Does this make any sense to anyone? And to make matters even more obscure, Haaretz is now reporting that even as the UN seeks permission from Israel to conduct a thorough investigation into Hamas' alleged sex crimes, the Israeli government is blocking access altogether.
So, we must ask--why is Israel obstructing this investigation and protecting Hamas?
The answer lies in what the investigation might reveal about Israel's own conduct. _Haaretz_ confirms that Israel fears a UN investigation into Hamas would instead expose the rape and sexual violence committed by Israeli forces against Palestinians, [reporting](https://substack.com/redirect/32ec8f3c-86d4-4501-af41-27b188d10504?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg):
> The UN's special representative on conflict-related sexual violence asked to investigate Hamas' alleged crimes on October 7 and towards the hostages, but Israel refused her request to access Israeli detention facilities to examine claims of IDF abuse. Israeli women's rights groups warn this could lead to Israel, instead of Hamas, being added to the UN's sexual violence blacklist
Clearly, Israel fears that a thorough UN investigation might not only absolve Hamas of the war crimes of rape and sexual assault, but affirm the Israeli military committed the war crime of rape and sexual assault. Again -- and I cannot emphasize this strongly enough -- these are not my opinions, but the reported statements of the Israeli government, Israeli prosecutors, Israeli women's rights organizations, and Israeli media. Why are U.S. politicians still funding Netanyahu despite this, and why is western media still silent about this?
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..YjBHh6PXgMysfq_AT3GbkZ5h6t7hRK50b98vHpmSrdg?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=154426158)
### **Evidence of Israeli War Crimes**
The evidence against the Israeli government for committing war crimes is damning, and in fact overwhelming. _ProPublica_ [reported](https://substack.com/redirect/0762d3fa-3eb9-4b64-8317-79be3123db7a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in April, 2024 that a special U.S. State Department panel recommended disqualifying several Israeli military and police units from receiving U.S. aid after reviewing allegations of serious human rights abuses, including the rape of Palestinian prisoners and children by the Israeli military. Secretary of State Antony Blinken refused to act on these recommendations, and instead [knowingly testified to the opposite to Congress](https://substack.com/redirect/08c9e12b-ae8d-41d7-a58d-af91225ce79c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). These abuses aren't hypothetical--they're backed by concrete reports and the include:
* Extrajudicial killings by Israeli Border Police.
* The death of an elderly Palestinian American man who was gagged and handcuffed by an Israeli battalion.
* The rape and torture of a Palestinian teenager accused of throwing rocks.

And none of this is second hand. We have video evidence confirming that Israeli forces raped Palestinian prisoners at Sde Teiman prison. The U.S. State Department acknowledged this video and yet refused to act or uphold the US Leahy Laws which forbids the US Government from sending arms to any nation credibly accused of war crimes. Instead, the U.S. State Department inexplicably called on the Israeli government to [investigate itself of rape](https://substack.com/redirect/0eef3c79-7d2a-4cbc-a39c-9f2d1d7bb8e1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and report back.
Can you imagine any logical scenario where a credibly accused rapist--with video evidence of the rape--is then empowered to investigate themselves?
### **MAGA 's Shield for Netanyahu**
I commend the International Criminal Court for filing arrest warrants for Hamas leaders and for Israeli leaders, including Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu. But now, adding to the outrage of Israel's aforementioned obstructionism into investigating rape allegations against Hamas, MAGA Republicans--and 45 Democrats--have voted to sanction the ICC for prosecuting Netanyahu for war crimes. On the very day of President Jimmy Carter's funeral, no less--[a president who boldly called out Israel's apartheid policies](https://substack.com/redirect/738aab27-5590-4b3d-a371-ebe096311770?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)--MAGA Republicans chose to shield Netanyahu of war crimes prosecution, and 45 Democrats inexplicably joined them.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/b342767b-ebee-462f-92c2-c91b2701c5ec?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
The 45 Democrats who voted with MAGA Republicans to protect Netanyahu from war crimes prosecution.
President Biden, meanwhile, has announced that one of his final acts in office will be to send [$8 billion more in military aid to Netanyahu's government](https://substack.com/redirect/9bd9daa7-783e-4ee6-9cbf-3a8ae28566b2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), even as the UN, Human Rights Watch, Amnesty International, and our own U.S. State Department confirm Israeli war crimes. This is not just a failure of justice--it's a moral abdication of duty. No one is above the law. The fact that Israel is obstructing investigations because they fear exposure of their own crimes is indefensible. And the fact that our US Congress is prioritizing protecting a credibly accused war criminal over the dozens of crises we face here at home is beyond reprehensible.
[Give a gift subscription](https://substack..YjBHh6PXgMysfq_AT3GbkZ5h6t7hRK50b98vHpmSrdg?&gift=true)
### **What Must Be Done To Uphold Justice?**
In conclusion, at a minimum we must demand the following:
1. **Immediate and transparent investigations** into all allegations of sexual violence and war crimes committed by both Hamas and Israeli forces. The entire narrative has focused on "_overwhelming evidence_ " of mass rape committed by Hamas. To suddenly claim "_there is no evidence_ " means either the Israeli government lied over the past 16 months, or the Israeli government is complicit in covering up crimes of mass rape. Which one is it?
2. **An end to U.S. military aid** to Israel until these investigations are completed and accountability is ensured, just as our Leahy Laws require. We cannot claim to be a country of laws and international rules based order, as Biden often states, and then violate our most foundational human rights laws as the Israeli government bombs and kills hundreds of thousands of Palestinians (per the Lancet study).
3. **Transparency from our elected officials** , especially those who voted to shield Netanyahu from prosecution. Why do they believe certain people are above the law? In a functioning democracy, no person is above the law. Israel cannot simultaneously be "the only democracy in the Middle East" and also have a head of state who is above the law. Our members of Congress cannot claim to uphold the US Constitution, which specifically condemns monarchial rule, and then demand monarchial powers be granted to their friends.

The bottom line is that these atrocities cannot be ignored. Justice for the victims--whether Palestinian or Israeli--requires confronting the truth, no matter how uncomfortable it may be. The failure of corporate media is just one more reason why this human rights newsletter exists. It's why I am grateful for your trust, support, and partnership. If you value my researched writing and want to stay informed on these critical issues, **subscribe below**. Together, we can hold the powerful accountable and demand a world where our standard is not partisanship or billionaire influence, but absolute justice for all people.

| [](https://substack.com/redirect/7272b6f4-9716-4676-a16c-7f9d1f3742b8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---

And why did 45 Democrats vote with MAGA Republicans to protect Netanyahu from war crimes prosecution? | | | Jan 10 | | | | | | | | | | | | In November 2023, Israel declared there was "" that Hamas had committed rape and sexual assault during the October 7 attacks. This narrative has since shaped the global discourse. But recent revelations have exposed unsettling truths that challenge this claim--challenges not by critics of Israel--but by Netanyahu and Israeli prosecutors themselves. The Israeli government is quietly retracting its earlier claims of mass and systemic rape, and corporate media is ignoring it altogether. Given our standard must remain absolute justice, it is critical we remain informed and get to the bottom of why Netanyahu is suddenly protecting Hamas. Let 's Address This. As Legacy media continues to fail to hold the powerful accountable, your support of Let 's Address This remains more critical than ever. Please become an active member of this community and subscribe. | | ---|---|--- The Silence of Prosecutors After 16 months of investigations, Israeli authorities have admitted that prosecutors have filed no allegations of rape or sexual assault against Hamas for the October 7 attacks. Prosecutor Moran Gaz is the former head of the security cases division at the Southern District Prosecutor's Office in Israel, and is a member of 'Team 7.10,' responsible for prosecuting rape and sexual assault crimes that occurred on 10/7. In an interview with Israeli media outlet Ynet News she admits that her department has "found no evidence to substantiate the claims of "mass" and "systematic" rape by Hamas." She want on to : In the end, we don't have any complainants. What was presented in the media compared to what will eventually come together will be entirely different... We approached women's rights organisations and asked for cooperation. They told us that no one had approached them. So, not only could her team find no evidence of mass or systemic rape, even with the help of women's rights organizations they could not find a single example to prosecute. Moran went on to state : [I] want to see everyone who crossed the fence stand trial and receive the death penalty. I don't have a drop of mercy. Sorry. But the path to bringing justice is slow and full of obstacles, especially with regard to sexual offenses. Unfortunately, it will be very difficult to prove them. In this matter, I would lower expectations. How did the Israeli government go from "overwhelming evidence " in November of 2023, to "we don 't have any complainants" after 16 months of investigations? This is a question no one in corporate media is asking, nor is it a question any American politician is demanding an answer to as the US Government continues to send billions in arms to Netanyahu. All this is in and of itself shocking, but the revelations don't end here. The Israeli government is not only refusing to act on its claims of mass and systemic rape on 10/7, they are now blocking the same UN investigations they demanded in November of 2023. | | ---|---|--- Blocking Accountability Israel's oldest newspaper, Haaretz , reports that Israel is actively blocking the UN from investigating sexual crimes allegedly committed by Hamas on October 7. A thorough investigation could place Hamas on the UN's blacklist of entities guilty of sexual violence--a critical step toward justice. In fact, dozens of organizations in Israel and globally made this exact demand in November, 2023. Jerusalem Post : The United Nations should declare Hamas's " a crime against humanity," according to a letter published Monday from more than 60 ideologically and religiously diverse American and international Jewish organizations. "Despite this overwhelming body of evidence, the UN has yet to list Hamas in the Annex to the Report of the Secretary-General as a party credibly suspected of committing or being responsible for patterns of rape or other forms of sexual violence in situations of armed conflict," the letter said. And following up on this demand, in a March, 2024 preliminary investigation the UN reported that while there were reasonable grounds to believe acts of sexual violence had occurred, more investigations would be needed, : While there are reasonable grounds to believe that conflict-related sexual violence occurred in the Nova music festival site, Route 232, and kibbutz Re'im, reported incidents of rape could not be verified in other locations. Concurrently, the team determined that at least two allegations of sexual violence in kibbutz Be 'eri -- widely reported in the media -- were unfounded. [emphasis added]. So to keep track, the narrative transitioned from "overwhelming evidence " in November of 2023, to "reasonable grounds but unfounded rape allegations " in March of 2024, to "we don 't have any complainants" in January of 2025. Does this make any sense to anyone? And to make matters even more obscure, Haaretz is now reporting that even as the UN seeks permission from Israel to conduct a thorough investigation into Hamas' alleged sex crimes, the Israeli government is blocking access altogether. So, we must ask--why is Israel obstructing this investigation and protecting Hamas? The answer lies in what the investigation might reveal about Israel's own conduct. Haaretz confirms that Israel fears a UN investigation into Hamas would instead expose the rape and sexual violence committed by Israeli forces against Palestinians, : The UN's special representative on conflict-related sexual violence asked to investigate Hamas' alleged crimes on October 7 and towards the hostages, but Israel refused her request to access Israeli detention facilities to examine claims of IDF abuse. Israeli women's rights groups warn this could lead to Israel, instead of Hamas, being added to the UN's sexual violence blacklist Clearly, Israel fears that a thorough UN investigation might not only absolve Hamas of the war crimes of rape and sexual assault, but affirm the Israeli military committed the war crime of rape and sexual assault. Again -- and I cannot emphasize this strongly enough -- these are not my opinions, but the reported statements of the Israeli government, Israeli prosecutors, Israeli women's rights organizations, and Israeli media. body:
[ stdin# ][IMG]

Peter Willis, more than
10,000 Our Revolution activists have joined our effort calling on Congress
to protect America’s most cherished and important public media stations
NPR and PBS from Trump and Musk’s massive funding cuts. [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/20910?t=1011&akid=7505%2E5885482%2E11-6g2 ]Can you add
your name to our urgent push to save NPR and PBS from Musk’s devastating
cuts?

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/20911?source=email-20250110&t=1012&akid=7505%2E5885482%2E11-6g2 ]ADD YOUR NAME

If you have a minute, please read our email from earlier this week
detailing Musk’s plans to destroy public media. Your help is urgently
needed!

- Our Revolution

-----Forwarded Message-----
To: dpwillis67@gmail.com
From: Save NPR
Subject: Petition: Save NPR and PBS

Peter Willis,

Trump and Musk have a new target: beloved public media stations NPR and
PBS.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/20911?t=1013&akid=7505%2E5885482%2E11-6g2 ][IMG]

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/20911?source=email-20250110&t=1014&akid=7505%2E5885482%2E11-6g2 ]After facing one Republican assault after the next in recent years, NPR
and PBS are already hanging on by a thread. And now Trump and Musk want to
completely gut the most trusted, unbiased source for news out there. Will
you urgently add your name to save PBS and NPR?

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/20911?source=email-20250110&t=1015&akid=7505%2E5885482%2E11-6g2 ]ADD YOUR NAME

Musk has floated lots of ideas to cut spending – eliminating entire
government departments and firing thousands. But one of the most concrete
things on his list is slashing HUNDREDS OF MILLIONS of dollars in annual
funding for PBS and NPR.

It’s not a new threat. Republicans have been waging a war on public
broadcasting for years, trying to do away with free, fact-based
independent journalism that is required by law to be objective and free
from corporate or political agendas.

When Trump tried to slash public media funding during his first term,
millions mobilized to stop him. Trump lost that fight, but this time could
be different as Republicans line up behind him and Musk pushes harder than
ever for major cuts.

We’re going to be ready.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/20911?source=email-20250110&t=1016&akid=7505%2E5885482%2E11-6g2 ]Together, we can fight back to save NPR and PBS – and win. But it will
only work if we mobilize in MASSIVE numbers, then relentlessly lobby
Congress to keep public media alive. That work starts NOW – will you add
your name to send a message straight to Congress?

In this polarized environment, free and independent media is more vital
than ever. And that’s exactly why Trump and his oligarch buddies have
settled on PBS and NPR as targets – without this vital public service,
they’ll be free to spread all the disinformation they desire.

Together, we can save one of our most important tools left to expose their
lies.

When we organize, we win.

Our Revolution

--------------------------------------------------------------------------

Our Revolution is the largest entirely grassroots funded progressive
group in America.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/13120?c0=email-20250110.gFWUYR&t=1008&akid=7505%2E5885482%2E11-6g2 ]Help us defend democracy and take the fight directly to the
establishment by committing to be a 2024 Movement Builder with a

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/13121?c0=email-20250110._cJOw4&t=1009&akid=7505%2E5885482%2E11-6g2 ]GIVE MONTHLY

name: Voter Action Project HQ
subject: They're trying to steal an election in plain sight
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Integrity First Voter Action Project, www
body:
Apparently the mantra of the vote suppressors is if you can't beat 'em, Team, just try and get their votes thrown out.
This may be happening in North Carolina but it's a problem everywhere. As Republican Justice Richard Dietz said in his scathing dissent, joining his lone Democratic colleague: “ the timing of Griffin’s claims speaks volumes about their substance. By waiting until after the votes were cast and the results tallied, Griffin seeks to retroactively rewrite the rules of the election to tilt the playing field in his favor .”
This isn't "election integrity" or whatever buzzwords opponents of voting rights use, this is blatant retroactive voter suppression. NC Newsline: Republicans on the NC Supreme Court block certification of the Democratic incumbent’s election [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?refcode=011025_1&amount=25]
All eyes are still on North Carolina, Team and for good reason.
Our state Supreme Court here in North Carolina just stepped in to stop the certification of the election for a justice on their court, which is a huge breach of power and a clearly partisan action in a state where judges are affiliated with political parties.
For background: On Election Day, incumbent Justice Allison Riggs (D) was reelected by a slim margin of a few hundred votes. Her disgruntled opponent, GOP Judge Jefferson Griffin, sued for and received two separate recounts -- both of which increased Riggs' lead.
Now Judge Griffin has sued to remove 60,000 voters' ballots from the count , even though they've been validated in the recount. The state Board of Elections dismissed his request , but now his partisan, would-be colleagues -- the court has a 5-2 Republican majority -- have stepped in to adjudicate the election and possibly kick off one of their own.
Justices Anita Earls (D) and Richard Dietz (R) dissented while Justice Riggs properly recused herself, so the judicial stay on election certification was put in place by a 4-2, mainly party-line vote.
The bottom line is that the partisan Supreme Court is stepping into an election for one of their own members to try and reverse the results of the vote because their party didn't win. Opponents of voting rights are trying to steal this election in plain sight.
We've got to make sure everyone knows about this egregious intervention before these votes are stolen and the voices of the people are silenced. Will you chip in $25 or whatever you can right now to help us spread the word and defend these votes in North Carolina and so many other votes across the country? >> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?refcode=011025_1&amount=25]

name: Josh Weil
subject: 81 days to kneecap Project 2025
body:
On April 1, we can stop Trump's MAGA agenda by winning back the House in this special election.
| | | | | [](https://wb7c9thbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001eeyif0hCHIPuFHQgUNdQTi3T8Kg2nN3yMhfvFkQa-39NLxwjAkQNSM_O8mbr_QWddjj8210qY5Zr0zzxOT0Gcy7l7FD_a4mnR4A87V_26upBn1Q6WXgEmYTR3TKS687FRafBx-tVSBqKlL2K-U1QHbZuyLabyZlio1CRpnbAwsEgB4jzZ6R3h9xhXFYNlQ_iHP6nbzc4VKA-nCxHuzLyWrORypxPagTl&c=WgTd_PuZ4Qmc7TUF9QYEeBnU3sXzMDpdpVLYB7qyfP7Rtu2h3szsyg==&ch=hi6ijB-Kh5ZGQPLNYSxkPtjCkOndVl6g7DlqZ2nGgCw0BN-rnrVKEw==)
---
| | | | Team,
As of today, we have 81 days to defeat Donald Trump's handpicked MAGA puppet in this special election--a crucial test coming at the perfect time: during Trump's first 100 days in office.
With this election, we have the chance to kneecap the Project 2025 agenda in Congress and place a sorely-needed check on the Trump White House.
If we win, the House will flip to Democrats. That means we will retake the majority. Democrats will be able to launch investigations, create special committees, and subpoena documents. And we can fight for truth and accountability at the exact moment Trump and Elon Musk will be sharpening their knives to dismantle the federal government to enrich themselves.
That is what is at stake, and it's why we need the biggest grassroots army we can muster to take on the entire MAGA universe. They know that failure here means disaster for Project 2025, so they won't be rolling over. But neither will we--not while we have anything to say about it.
---
